rows.Key Lesson: Multi‑tranche sovereign cat bonds can map financing to peril and geography
rows.Explanation: Inference based on source: Mexico repeatedly used separate tranches for earthquakes, Atlantic storms, and Pacific storms across different programs
rows.Relevance for Sovereign Finance: Enables targeted fiscal protection and clearer budgeting of contingent liabilities
rows.Possible Relevance for Israel: Inference based on source: Israel could structure earthquake vs. other hazards into separate layers/instruments

rows.Key Lesson: Parametric design supports tiered payouts and rapid liquidity
rows.Explanation: The Mexico programs describe explicit tiering (e.g., 25/50/75/100) and storm-track/central-pressure mechanics
rows.Relevance for Sovereign Finance: Pre‑agreed payout rules reduce post-event uncertainty and speed liquidity
rows.Possible Relevance for Israel: Inference based on source: relevant to rapid-response funding after major seismic events

rows.Key Lesson: Public insurer / fund intermediation can operationalize sovereign coverage
rows.Explanation: The PDFs describe chains including FONDEN trustees, Agroasemex, Munich Re / Swiss Re and IBRD
rows.Relevance for Sovereign Finance: Helps translate capital-markets coverage into sovereign budget execution
rows.Possible Relevance for Israel: Inference based on source: Israel could consider an institutional conduit to receive/route proceeds
